channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Toronto Tempo's Turnover Plague Costs Them in Loss to Atlanta Dream
item.description: The Toronto Raptors' women's basketball team, the Toronto Tempo, suffered an 111-92 loss to the Atlanta Dream, exacerbated by 20 costly turnovers that the Dream converted into 29 points. Coach Sandy B...

item.title: Ancient Egyptian royal women's skeletal evidence suggests weapon use in daily life
item.description: A new study of royal skeletons from ancient Egypt's Twelfth Dynasty suggests that women, including princesses, may have regularly used weapons. The research, led by Zaineb Hashesh from the University ...

item.title: Delhi Police moves hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk to hospital amid protest tensions
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike since June 28, was forcibly removed from the Jantar Mantar protest site by Delhi Police on Saturday. The police claimed he was shifted to a hos...

item.title: Zanzibar Urges Contractors to Address Project Delays
item.description: The article highlights Zanzibar's recent warning to contractors regarding ongoing project delays. Officials have emphasized the need for timely completion of infrastructure projects to avoid further e...

item.title: Nigeria aims to establish a thriving games economy through Lagos Games Week 2026
item.description: The article discusses Nigeria's efforts to develop a robust games economy through Lagos Games Week 2026. The event, supported by the French Embassy, brought together developers, investors, and governm...

item.title: Hannah Waddingham Reflects on Career, Sexism, and New Projects at 51
item.description: Hannah Waddingham, renowned for her role as Rebecca Welton in the hit TV series Ted Lasso, discusses her career trajectory, challenges faced as a woman in the entertainment industry, and her recent ve...

item.title: Weekly Photo Highlights from The Age's Staff Photographers
item.description: The article features a curated collection of 17 photographs taken by The Age's award-winning staff photographers and regular contributors, capturing significant moments from July 18, 2026. The images ...

item.title: Hunger-striking activist Sonam Wangchuk hospitalised after Delhi police intervention
item.description: Sonam Wangchuk, a 59-year-old activist, was removed from his protest site in Delhi by police and admitted to hospital after his health deteriorated during a 21-day hunger strike. The action followed h...

item.title: Growing Divide Among U.S. Democrats Over Israel Support Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Over 100 U.S. House Democrats voted to cut military aid to Israel, exposing a widening party divide ahead of the midterm elections. This move highlights a generational shift within the Democratic Part...

item.title: Nadia Mukami Opens Up About Remarriage and Relationship Expectations After Split from Arrow Bwoy
item.description: Nadia Mukami, the Kenyan singer who split from musician Arrow Bwoy, shared her thoughts on love and remarriage during a podcast interview. She revealed she's open to finding new love but outlined spec...

item.title: Mamdani Calls for Global Solidarity in Honor of Mandela's Legacy Against Injustice
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani paid tribute to Nelson Mandela's enduring legacy, emphasizing the importance of solidarity in combating injustice. Reflecting on Mandela's lifelong struggle against ...

item.title: Zanzibar Warns Contractors About Project Delays
item.description: The article discusses concerns raised by Zanzibar authorities regarding delays in construction projects. Contractors are being urged to adhere to timelines to avoid penalties. The piece highlights the...

item.title: Shakthisree Gopalan's Journey Through Music, Mental Health, and Creative Exploration
item.description: Singer-songwriter Shakthisree Gopalan reflects on her latest independent release 'Old Friend', a deeply personal track that explores themes of self-acceptance, neurodivergence, and mental health. Penn...

item.title: Lucas Herbert takes early lead at British Open after historic 62
item.description: Lucas Herbert has surged to the halfway mark at the 154th British Open after carding a record-equalling 62 in the first round at Royal Birkdale. The Australian star, who is making his major championsh...

item.title: NCRPO: 4 Groups Seek Permits for Marcos' SONA 2026 Rallies
item.description: At least four groups have applied for permits to hold rallies in Quezon City during President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr.'s fifth State of the Nation Address (SONA) on July 27, 2026. The National ...

item.title: Boeing Completes Regulatory Approval for Factory Operations
item.description: Boeing has successfully obtained key regulatory approvals for its factory operations, marking a significant step toward resuming production. The milestone comes after years of delays and scrutiny due ...

item.title: Ananse trains African entrepreneurs to boost fashion industry
item.description: The article highlights how Ananse Centre for Design's Fashion Incubator Scholarship Programme is empowering over 7,000 creatives across Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Senegal, Côte d’Ivoire, and South Africa....

item.title: Public Protector Report Highlights Systemic Failures in Gauteng's Food Safety System
item.description: The Public Protector's report reveals that Gauteng's food safety system is fundamentally compromised due to systemic governance failures, corruption, and weak enforcement. Despite measures taken after...

item.title: First Nations Chiefs Urge Federal and Provincial Governments to Protect Treaty Rights Amid Alberta Separatism
item.description: First Nations chiefs from across Canada have passed a resolution demanding the federal and provincial governments protect treaty rights against threats posed by Alberta's separatism. The Assembly of F...

item.title: Bauchi State Governorship Race 2027: Key Candidates and Political Dynamics
